text:
policy 
regarding the benchmark criteria that should be used when determining whether inpatient admission is 
reasonable and payable under Medicare Part A. In general, the Two-Midnight rule stated that Inpatient 
admissions will generally be payable under Part A if the admitting practitioner expected the patient to 
require a hospital stay that crossed two midnights and the medical record supports that reasonable 
expectation.
